title: pimelea lehmanniana subsp. nervosa
intro: Pimelea lehmanniana subsp. nervosa, also known as Nervous Rice Flower or Nervous Pimelea, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Thymelaeaceae. It has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and aiding in wound healing. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Pimelea lehmanniana subsp. nervosa are often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices.
